seo.meta_title: Auth.js vs Better Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins?
seo.meta_description: Looking for Auth.js vs Better Auth for your Next.js app? We compare pricing (Free (Open Source) vs Free (Open Source)), DX scores, setup time, and give you our honest pick.
seo.og_title: Auth.js vs Better Auth — Which Next.js Auth Tool Wins?
seo.og_description: Auth.js (4.6★) vs Better Auth (4.5★). Medium learning curve vs Medium. Find your ideal Next.js auth solution.

hero.headline: Auth.js vs Better Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Auth.js and Better Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Both Auth.js and Better Auth are solid choices for Next.js authentication, but they serve different needs. Auth.js edges ahead with a stronger Next.js integration score (8/10 vs 8/10). Where Better Auth shines is Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. If you are optimizing for developer experience in the App Router, Auth.js is the safer bet.
verdict_text: For most Next.js developers building production apps, we recommend Auth.js. It offers better Next.js integration (8/10 vs 8/10), a medium learning curve, and a strong feature set that aligns well with App Router patterns. Better Auth remains a solid choice — especially if yes, fully open source with self-hosting is important to your stack.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, fully open source with self-hosting

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

pros: Fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage limits
Self-hosted on your own infrastructure - complete data control
Growing community and active development on GitHub
cons: Requires database setup and self-hosting infrastructure
Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 or Clerk
Fewer pre-built UI components compared to managed alternatives

faq.question: Should I choose Auth.js or Better Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Auth.js scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Better Auth scores 8/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source or Yes, fully open source with self-hosting is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Better Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Better Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or Better Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Better Auth has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 30 minutes. Auth.js is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Auth.js and Better Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Auth.js and Better Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Auth.js or Better Auth?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.6★. Better Auth has a Medium learning curve, 8/10, and 4.5★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
